title: Castle in the Air - Chapter Four: Which concerns marriage and prophecy (Fragment 3)
text:
‚ÄúWhy, Abdullah! How prompt you are and how smart you look!‚Äù she said, and her manner said she had expected him to arrive late and in rags.

‚ÄúHe looks almost as if he were dressed for his wedding!‚Äù Assif said, advancing, too, with a smile on his thin, bad-tempered face.

It was so rare to see Assif smiling that Abdullah thought for a moment that Assif had ricked his neck and was grimacing with pain. Then Hakim sniggered, which made Abdullah realize what Assif had just said. To his annoyance, he found he was blushing furiously. He was forced to bow politely in order to hide his face.

‚ÄúThere‚Äôs no need to make the boy blush!‚Äù Fatima cried. That, of course, made Abdullah‚Äôs blush worse. ‚ÄúAbdullah, what is this rumor we hear that you are suddenly planning to deal in pictures?‚Äù

‚ÄúAnd selling the best of your stock to make room for the pictures,‚Äù added Hakim.

Abdullah ceased to blush. He saw he had been summoned here to be criticized. He was sure of it when Assif added reproachfully, ‚ÄúOur feelings are somewhat hurt, son of my father‚Äôs niece‚Äôs husband, that you did not seem to think we could oblige you by taking a few carpets off your hands.‚Äù

‚ÄúDear relatives,‚Äù said Abdullah. ‚ÄúI could not, of course, sell you my carpets. My aim was to make a profit, and I could hardly mulct you, whom my father loved.‚Äù He was so annoyed that he turned around to go away again, only to find that Hakim had quietly shut and barred the doors.

‚ÄúNo need to stay open,‚Äù Hakim said. ‚ÄúLet us be just family here.‚Äù

‚ÄúThe poor boy!‚Äù said Fatima. ‚ÄúNever has he had more need of a family to keep his mind in order!‚Äù

‚ÄúYes, indeed,‚Äù said Assif. ‚ÄúAbdullah, some rumors in the Bazaar state that you have gone mad. We do not like this.‚Äù

‚ÄúHe‚Äôs certainly been behaving oddly,‚Äù Hakim agreed. ‚ÄúWe don‚Äôt like such talk connected to a respectable family like ours.‚Äù

This was worse than usual. Abdullah said, ‚ÄúThere is nothing wrong with my mind. I know just what I am doing. And my aim is to cease giving you any chance to criticize me, probably by tomorrow. Meanwhile, Hakim told me to come here because you have found the prophecy that was made at my birth. Is this correct, or was it merely an excuse?‚Äù He had never been so rude to his father‚Äôs first wife‚Äôs relations before, but he was angry enough to feel they deserved it.

Oddly enough, instead of being angry with Abdullah in return, all three of his father‚Äôs first wife‚Äôs relations began hurrying excitedly around the emporium.

‚ÄúNow where is that box?‚Äù said Fatima.

‚ÄúFind it, find it!‚Äù said Assif. ‚ÄúIt is the very words of the fortune-teller his poor father brought to the bedside of his second wife an hour after Abdullah‚Äôs birth. He must see it!‚Äù

‚ÄúWritten in your own father‚Äôs hand,‚Äù Hakim said to Abdullah. ‚ÄúThe greatest treasure for you.‚Äù

‚Äú Here it is!‚Äù said Fatima, triumphantly pulling a carved wooden box off a high shelf. She gave the box to Assif, who thrust it into Abdullah‚Äôs hands.

‚ÄúOpen it, open it!‚Äù they all three cried excitedly.

Abdullah put the box down on the purple cashier‚Äôs table and sprang the catch. The lid went back, bringing a musty smell from inside, which was perfectly plain and empty apart from a folded yellowish paper.

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones
meta.sectionTitle: Chapter Four: Which concerns marriage and prophecy

vocabulary.definition: Slightly angry or irritated.
vocabulary.example: He was so annoyed that he turned around to go away again.

vocabulary.definition: Easily annoyed or made angry.
vocabulary.example: ...with a smile on his thin, bad-tempered face.

vocabulary.definition: Acting in an unusual or strange way.
vocabulary.example: He‚Äôs certainly been behaving oddly.

vocabulary.definition: Decorated by cutting into the surface.
vocabulary.example: A carved wooden box.

vocabulary.definition: To stop doing something.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah ceased to blush.

vocabulary.definition: Relationships or links between people or things.
vocabulary.example: Relations here refers to family connection as important to the narrative.

vocabulary.definition: Indicate the faults of someone or something in a disapproving way.
vocabulary.example: He was sure of it when Assif added reproachfully... to be criticized.

vocabulary.definition: A large retail store selling a wide variety of goods.
vocabulary.example: They began hurrying excitedly around the emporium.

vocabulary.definition: A reason or explanation put forward to justify an action.
vocabulary.example: Is this correct, or was it merely an excuse?

vocabulary.definition: A person who predicts the future by magical or supernatural means.
vocabulary.example: It is the very words of the fortune-teller his poor father brought to the bedside...

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows intense anger or excitement.
vocabulary.example: He found he was blushing furiously.

vocabulary.definition: Making a twisted facial expression showing pain or disgust.
vocabulary.example: Assif had ricked his neck and was grimacing with pain.

vocabulary.definition: To defraud or swindle someone of something, especially money.
vocabulary.example: I could hardly mulct you, whom my father loved.

vocabulary.definition: Having a stale, moldy, or damp smell.
vocabulary.example: The lid went back, bringing a musty smell from inside.

vocabulary.definition: Make someone legally or morally bound to do something; do a favour for.
vocabulary.example: ...think we could oblige you by taking a few carpets off your hands.

vocabulary.definition: Done without delay; immediate.
vocabulary.example: Why, Abdullah! How prompt you are and how smart you look!

vocabulary.definition: Expressing disapproval or disappointment.
vocabulary.example: Assif added reproachfully, ‚ÄúOur feelings are somewhat hurt...‚Äù

vocabulary.definition: Regarded by society to be good, proper, or correct.
vocabulary.example: ...talk connected to a respectable family like ours.

vocabulary.definition: Offensively impolite or ill-mannered.
vocabulary.example: He had never been so rude to his father‚Äôs first wife‚Äôs relations before.

vocabulary.definition: To close and lock a door securely.
vocabulary.example: Hakim had quietly shut and barred the doors.

vocabulary.definition: Neat and stylish in appearance.
vocabulary.example: How smart you look!

vocabulary.definition: To laugh in a half-suppressed, typically scornful way.
vocabulary.example: Then Hakim sniggered, which made Abdullah realize what Assif had just said.

vocabulary.definition: A quantity of precious metals, gems, or other valuable objects.
vocabulary.example: The greatest treasure for you.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows great satisfaction or joy at a victory or achievement.
vocabulary.example: Fatima, triumphantly pulling a carved wooden box off a high shelf.
